mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening, resulting in negative equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint high retail sales and inflation periods yield positive returns in greater than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals immediate supply-side weakness that triggers short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions coincide with positive 5-day returns in greater than 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods show negative forward returns in greater than 50% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts exceeding 10% quarter-over-quarter alongside contracting real GDP confirms a broad demand collapse, driving negative quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% QoQ and GDPC1 growth < 0%
failure_condition: Housing and GDP contractions fail to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes exceeding 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising core inflation alongside a static or rising federal funds rate creates a real rate squeeze that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation and high rates periods yield positive returns in greater than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling employment indicates capital substitution that boosts short-term corporate margins, generating positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The output-employment divergence yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 50% of cases.
